decisions.title: Why Request-ID Correlation
decisions.description: Every message between agents carries a unique request ID. When an agent responds, it includes the original request ID so the sender can match the response to the request. Without this, an agent that sends multiple requests cannot determine which response belongs to which request, especially when responses arrive out of order.
decisions.alternatives: Sequential request-response (wait for each response before sending the next request) avoids the correlation problem but serializes all communication, eliminating parallelism. Channel-based isolation (one channel per conversation) works but multiplies the number of mailbox files. Request IDs are the standard solution used in HTTP, RPC, and message queues.

zh.title: 为什么需要请求 ID 关联
zh.description: Agent 之间的每条消息都携带唯一的请求 ID。当 Agent 回复时，会附上原始请求 ID，这样发送者能将响应与请求对应起来。没有这个机制，一个发送了多个请求的 Agent 无法判断哪个响应对应哪个请求，特别是当响应乱序到达时。

decisions.title: Why FSM over Ad-Hoc Messaging
decisions.description: Each multi-step protocol (like code review: submit -> review -> revise -> approve) is modeled as a finite state machine. The current state determines which messages are valid and what transitions are possible. This prevents invalid message sequences and makes the protocol self-documenting. An agent can look at the FSM definition to understand the entire collaboration workflow.
decisions.alternatives: Free-form messaging (agents send whatever they want) is flexible but leads to deadlocks, missed steps, and inconsistent behavior. A full workflow engine is too heavyweight. FSMs capture the essential sequencing constraints without over-engineering the solution.

zh.title: 为什么用有限状态机而非随意消息传递
zh.description: 每个多步骤协议（如代码审查：提交 -> 审查 -> 修改 -> 批准）被建模为有限状态机（FSM）。当前状态决定了哪些消息有效、哪些状态转换是可能的。这防止了无效的消息序列，并使协议具有自文档化特性。Agent 可以查看 FSM 定义来理解完整的协作流程。

decisions.title: Why Protocol Versioning from Day One
decisions.description: Each protocol message includes a version field. This allows agents running different versions of the protocol to detect incompatibilities and degrade gracefully rather than silently producing wrong results. In a team of autonomous agents, you cannot upgrade all agents simultaneously — version awareness is essential for safe evolution.
decisions.alternatives: Ignoring versioning works until the first breaking change, at which point debugging becomes extremely difficult. Requiring all agents to upgrade simultaneously is impractical in long-running systems. Including version metadata from the start costs almost nothing and prevents future headaches.

zh.title: 为什么从第一天就引入协议版本控制
zh.description: 每条协议消息都包含版本字段。这使得运行不同协议版本的 Agent 能够检测不兼容性，优雅降级而非默默产生错误结果。在自主 Agent 团队中，你无法同时升级所有 Agent——版本感知对于安全演进是必不可少的。
